207276.explanation: Additional support for informal caregivers is essential to prevent overload and postpone expensive professional care. Informal caregivers are the indispensable backbone of our healthcare. By investing in respite care and advice, they remain healthy and local care facilities affordable.

207277.explanation: Welfare facilities are crucial to combat loneliness and social inequality. Expansion prevents minor complaints from escalating into costly specialist care. Investing in meeting places and prevention keeps society vital and saves the municipality money in the long term.

207278.explanation: Neighborhood initiatives are crucial to fight loneliness and strengthen social cohesion. Additional subsidies encourage residents to take responsibility for their neighborhood themselves. This increases well-being, prevents exclusion, and reduces demand for professional care over time.

207279.explanation: Meeting places are crucial to prevent social isolation and loneliness among the elderly. They promote an active lifestyle and mutual support, which lowers pressure on professional care. Investing in these places strengthens social cohesion and livability in the neighborhood.

207280.explanation: A larger animal shelter is needed for proper care of stray animals and public safety. Municipal support ensures animal welfare and relieves public spaces. Moreover, the shelter fulfills a social role through volunteer work and education, contributing to a caring municipality.

207221.explanation: It is important that the municipality involves residents in important issues. A referendum can help with this. However, it must never become a tool for politicians to avoid their responsibility. Politicians must continue to make clear choices themselves and take responsibility for them.

207222.explanation: 50PLUS believes that residents should have a say in important decisions. Therefore, 's-Hertogenbosch should organize citizen assemblies more often. Residents can give advice to the council and be taken seriously. Based on: City, Governance and Communication – participation, listening to residents, citizen assemblies.

207223.explanation: 50PLUS wants an ombudsman for the elderly and an alderman who assesses policies based on their impact on the elderly and people with disabilities. This way, signals are picked up and their interests are considered in decisions. See: Care and Welfare – people-centered policy, fixed contact points, inclusivity.

207224.explanation: 50PLUS believes residents should co-decide on the budget for their neighborhood or village. This way, the money better matches what residents find necessary and involvement increases. See: City, Governance and Communication – participation, engaged residents, strong neighborhoods.

207225.explanation: 50PLUS believes that priority in issuing building land should go to companies that create jobs in 's-Hertogenbosch. This strengthens the local economy and employment. See: Economy, Work and Income – local jobs, strong economy, support for entrepreneurs.

207226.explanation: 50PLUS wants to impose as few additional rules as possible on businesses beyond national regulations. Only if local circumstances really require it, tailored measures may be necessary. This keeps entrepreneurship feasible and fair. See: Economy, Work and Income – less regulatory pressure, room for entrepreneurs, tailored approach.

207227.explanation: 50PLUS believes single-person households should pay less for waste collection charges. This is fairer because they produce less waste and often have lower incomes. See: Poverty Reduction and Underprotection – fair burdens, protection of low incomes.

207229.explanation: 50PLUS believes there should be less advertising in public spaces. This creates calm, clarity, and a pleasant living environment for residents, especially for the elderly. Based on: Housing and Living Together – livable neighborhoods, safe and pleasant public space.

207231.explanation: 50PLUS believes the dog tax should be abolished. This tax is unfair and mostly affects people with low or fixed incomes. Maintenance of public spaces should be part of general provisions. See: Poverty Reduction and Underprotection – fair burdens, preventing extra tax pressure!

207232.explanation: 50PLUS believes new business parks are only desirable if they provide local jobs and do not harm the living environment or nature. Quality and integration take precedence over quantity. Based on: Economy, Work and Income – local employment, sustainable economy.

207233.explanation: 50PLUS believes the municipality should more strictly supervise abuses in housing labor migrants. Everyone has the right to safe, healthy, and humane living conditions. Based on: Housing and Living Together – safe living environment, decent housing for everyone.

207234.explanation: 50PLUS believes asylum reception should be fairly distributed over multiple locations within the municipality. This keeps reception manageable and prevents excessive pressure on neighborhoods. Based on: Housing and Living Together – livable neighborhoods, balanced distribution, social cohesion.

207235.explanation: 50PLUS believes status holders have the right to reception and safety. Prioritizing social rental housing does not solve housing shortages and increases tensions. The cause lies in years of insufficient construction; the municipality must focus on building. See: Housing and Living Together – affordable housing, fair distribution, strong neighborhoods.

207237.explanation: 50PLUS believes dumping waste should be tackled more strictly. At the same time, the municipality must keep costs affordable for residents because too high charges can actually cause illegal dumping. Based on: Housing and Living Together – clean and livable neighborhoods, fair burdens for residents.

207238.explanation: 50PLUS believes large events belong in the city center and contribute to liveliness and the local economy. However, a good balance is needed, with attention to livability for residents, such as noise, crowds, and accessibility.

207239.explanation: 50PLUS believes the municipality should spend more money on greenery in neighborhoods. Green spaces provide a pleasant living environment, health benefits, and opportunities for social interaction for young and old. Based on: Housing and Living Together – livable neighborhoods, healthy and safe public space.

207240.explanation: 50PLUS believes extended opening hours for restaurants and cafés are possible as long as the livability for neighbors is protected. Tailoring per location is important. Based on: Economy, Work and Income and Housing and Living Together – space for entrepreneurs, livable neighborhoods.

207241.explanation: 50PLUS believes the municipality should invest more in neighborhood maintenance. This also includes residents' own responsibility: with municipal support, they should be able to make their neighborhood greener according to a neighborhood plan. See: Housing and Living Together – livable neighborhoods, involvement, cooperation between neighborhood and municipality.

207242.explanation: 50PLUS believes the municipality of ’s-Hertogenbosch should assess all policies on sustainability. This ensures choices that are good for the environment, living environment, and future generations. Based on: Energy – sustainable, affordable and future-proof policy.

207243.explanation: 50PLUS believes investing in sports facilities is important, with more attention to residents' life phases. The municipality already has many sports facilities; the offerings should especially suit both young and old and remain well accessible. See: Culture and Sport – sport for everyone, appropriate for every age.

207244.explanation: 50PLUS believes sports facilities should be used more often by multiple clubs. This better utilizes the facilities and ensures careful use of taxpayers' money. Based on: Culture and Sport – accessible sports offerings, efficient use of facilities.

207245.explanation: 50PLUS believes culture is important for the livability and attractiveness of the city. Therefore, structurally cutting funding for cultural institutions is inappropriate; careful and effective spending of subsidies is necessary. See: Culture and Sport – culture as a connecting force and location factor.

207246.explanation: 50PLUS believes that new buildings should include space for music and culture, preferably in multifunctional buildings. Den Bosch already has many locations; new construction should strengthen and make good use of this supply. Based on: Culture and Sport – culture nearby, efficient use of facilities.

207247.explanation: 50PLUS is open to a new major attraction in the Citadelpoort, provided it fits the city and contributes to the economy, culture, and livability without nuisance for residents. Based on: Culture and Sport and Tourism and Recreation – attractiveness of the city, balance with livability.

207248.explanation: 50PLUS believes the municipality should allocate funds for school swimming. Swimming is important for children's safety and health and prevents unequal opportunities. Based on: Equal Opportunities and Broad Prosperity – equal chances, health and safety for everyone.

207249.explanation: 50PLUS believes more enforcement officers (boa’s) are needed in ’s-Hertogenbosch. Visible and approachable enforcement increases safety and the feeling of trust in the neighborhood. Based on: Safety – visible enforcement, safe and livable neighborhoods.

207250.explanation: 50PLUS believes the municipality should prepare for crisis situations by designing public spaces, such as stations, multifunctionally so they can also serve as shelters in emergencies. This increases residents' safety. See: Safety – preparation, protection, preparedness, prevention.

207251.explanation: 50PLUS believes the municipality should invest more in youth work. Good guidance and meeting opportunities prevent problems, increase opportunities, and strengthen safety and cohesion in neighborhoods. Based on: Safety and Equal Opportunities and Broad Prosperity – prevention, equal chances, and strong neighborhoods.

207252.explanation: 50PLUS believes that camera surveillance can be used in places with a lot of nuisance. This increases safety and helps with enforcement, respecting privacy and clear rules. Based on: Safety – targeted prevention, safe neighborhoods, visible enforcement.

207253.explanation: 50PLUS believes that social housing applicants from ’s-Hertogenbosch should be given priority as much as possible. This keeps the connection with the city strong and housing fair and livable. Based on: Housing and Living Together – affordable housing, strong neighborhoods, local connection.

207254.explanation: 50PLUS believes that building in rural areas is only possible if done carefully, respecting nature and the landscape. This should apply to all homes, including social housing, not just expensive owner-occupied houses. Based on: Housing and Living Together – affordable housing, careful building, livability.

207255.explanation: 50PLUS believes that elderly people moving to smaller homes have the right to relocation compensation. Moving should not lead to higher rent. Mobility should fairly pay off: lower rent in the new home and a moving allowance. Vacated homes yield more. See: Housing and Living Together – affordable housing, mobility.

207256.explanation: The obligation to occupy a property oneself encourages use of buildings and helps combat housing shortages and deterioration. Based on: Housing and Living Together – tackling vacancy, livable neighborhoods, better use of space.

207257.explanation: 50PLUS believes that high-rise buildings outside the historic city center are possible, provided this is done carefully and fits the environment. This way, we create housing without damaging the city's character. Based on: Housing and Living Together – careful building, affordable housing, heritage protection.

207259.explanation: 50PLUS believes that at least 40% of new build should be social rental housing. This ensures affordable homes and more opportunities for starters and elderly, above the national average of 28%. See: Housing and Living Together – affordable housing, balanced housing development, mobility.

207260.explanation: 50PLUS believes that those who buy a home should, in principle, live in it themselves. We must prevent speculation and ensure homes remain available for residents. See: Housing and Living Together – affordable housing, fair housing market, strong neighborhoods.

207261.explanation: 50PLUS believes that vulnerable neighborhoods may receive extra funding. This should be targeted and temporary, so neighborhoods are strengthened and no lasting disadvantage arises. Based on: Equal Opportunities and Broad Prosperity – equal chances, strong neighborhoods.

207262.explanation: 50PLUS believes the Bossche Pas should be expanded so that people with low incomes can participate in sports and culture. The pass should not be stigmatizing. Based on: Poverty Reduction and Underprotection – participation for everyone, dignity.

207263.explanation: 50PLUS wants to prevent abuse of poverty schemes, with controls that are humane and proportional. No witch hunts or punishments for minor mistakes; trust and assistance come first. Based on: Poverty Reduction and Underprotection – people-centered policy, dignity.

207264.explanation: 50PLUS believes support for the unemployed should focus on finding suitable paid work. With respectful guidance, we help people move forward, without punishment or judgment if someone is temporarily out of work. Based on: Economy, Work and Income – suitable work, people-centered support.

207265.explanation: 50PLUS wants more low-income people to make use of poverty schemes. At the same time, the emphasis is on prevention: good guidance to work and preventing debts. Based on: Poverty Reduction and Underprotection – accessibility, prevention and support.

207266.explanation: 50PLUS wants to reconsider the decision on the zero-emission zone. Protection of health is paramount. At the same time, measures must be fair, feasible and implemented with support. Based on: Energy and Care and Welfare – health, sustainability and just policy.

207267.explanation: 50PLUS believes the city center of Den Bosch must remain accessible by car. This is important for elderly people, less mobile residents and entrepreneurs, alongside good alternative transport options. See: Housing and Living Together and Economy, Work and Income – accessibility, inclusivity and strong city center.

207268.explanation: 50PLUS is open to an eastern access road for Rosmalen and the eastern side of the municipality, provided accessibility improves and livability and nature are protected. Based on: Housing and Living Together and Economy, Work and Income – accessibility and careful consideration.

207269.explanation: 50PLUS believes the municipality should advocate for a train station at Maaspoort. This improves accessibility, sustainability and mobility for residents, especially the elderly and commuters. See: Housing and Living Together and Economy, Work and Income – accessibility, livable neighborhoods and good connections.

207271.explanation: 50PLUS is not in favor of restricting through traffic as a principle. Accessibility is paramount, with room for customized solutions to improve livability, for example in the evenings and at night. Based on: Housing and Living Together and Economy, Work and Income – accessibility and tailored solutions.

207272.explanation: 50PLUS believes the municipality should invest in fast and safe cycling connections. This improves accessibility, health and sustainable mobility for young and old. Based on: Housing and Living Together and Energy – accessibility, health and sustainability.

207274.explanation: 50PLUS believes people should, where possible, be able to choose a caregiver who fits their religion or culture. The quality and availability of care and the health of the care recipient always come first. Based on: Care and Welfare – people-centered care, quality and accessibility.

207275.explanation: 50PLUS believes fixed care professionals per neighborhood are important. This provides continuity and trust, especially for elderly people with lighter care needs (ZZP 1–3). Based on: Care and Welfare – care nearby and fixed points of contact.

207276.explanation: 50PLUS believes informal caregivers should receive better support. Not only financially, but especially through good facilities such as respite care, so informal care remains sustainable. Based on: Care and Welfare – support for informal caregivers and respite care.

207277.explanation: 50PLUS opposes stopping the expansion of welfare facilities. They are needed for both elderly and young people, prevent problems and strengthen youth care through early support. Based on: Care and Welfare – prevention and welfare nearby and support for all ages!

207278.explanation: 50PLUS believes neighborhood initiatives that strengthen contact between residents deserve extra support. This can be done by allowing initiatives to use community centers and neighborhood facilities for free or at low cost. See: Housing and Living Together and Care and Welfare – social cohesion and strong neighborhoods.

207279.explanation: 50PLUS believes the municipality should invest in meeting places. Meeting others promotes health and prevents loneliness, for elderly people as well as for those with long covid or Q-fever, with space for conversation and advice. Based on: Care and Welfare – prevention, meeting and support nearby.

207280.explanation: 50PLUS believes that co-paying for the expansion of an animal shelter does not obviously fit within the municipal task. The municipality is responsible for animal rescue, not for general expansion. See: Housing and Living Together – clear responsibilities and careful use of public funds.

207221.explanation: Election promises are not always fulfilled. With referenda, residents have direct influence, instead of indirect (via a representative). The will of the population is best respected through a referendum on important issues.

207222.explanation: Referenda are better, more democratic and fairer.

207223.explanation: FVD wants good care for the elderly in the municipality, but an ombudsman is not necessary for that.

207225.explanation: Building land must benefit the residents and businesses, not data centers for foreign companies, for example.

207226.explanation: Entrepreneurship is already strongly discouraged in the Netherlands, says FVD. ’s-Hertogenbosch must not increase regulatory pressure but give entrepreneurs room to operate.

207227.explanation: Paying less tax is always good, unless others have to pay more to make up the difference.

207229.explanation: The municipality should not overly hinder entrepreneurs with too many advertising rules. Yet FVD also finds the streetscape important; excessive advertisements do not beautify the centers.

207232.explanation: FVD wants sufficient space for entrepreneurs. We modernize outdated business parks and improve their accessibility, so companies can remain and grow here.

207233.explanation: The rules concerning housing of labor migrants are clear. There is no reason to justify stricter enforcement of these rules or to spend money and manpower on it.

207234.explanation: Asylum reception in ’s-Hertogenbosch must be minimized. FVD does not want new reception centers and wants existing centers closed.

207235.explanation: Housing priority for residents of Den Bosch (Dutch people), not outsiders.

207237.explanation: FVD wants a clean municipality. Both in city and village centers and in nature and outer districts.

207238.explanation: 's-Hertogenbosch is characterized by a vibrant city center with hospitality establishments and events. The municipality consistently demonstrates good management of this. There is no reason to limit the number of events.

207239.explanation: Sufficient green space is important for a beautiful living environment.

207240.explanation: More freedom for entrepreneurs to decide how to manage their own businesses.

207241.explanation: Environmental care starts close to home. Not with pointless global ambitions that municipalities cannot influence, but with attention to the local living environment: clean streets, greenery, well-maintained public spaces, and a pleasant residential area.

207242.explanation: No forced 'sustainability'. In practice, this means: more taxes and bureaucracy for unnecessary CO2 and nitrogen reduction targets.

207243.explanation: Sports and community life are not secondary matters but essential parts of health and social cohesion. Associations bring generations together and are a natural remedy against loneliness. They deserve support and space.

207245.explanation: 's-Hertogenbosch is the cultural city of the South. We should be proud of that. Naturally, however, FVD does NOT want more money flowing to left-wing cultural institutions that are not concerned with the culture and history of Den Bosch, or even Dutch culture in general.

207246.explanation: For each new residential area, there should be an assessment of whether the supply and demand for associations are balanced. FVD wants tailored solutions. Fewer rules for building.

207247.explanation: FVD believes that historical buildings and locations in the municipality, such as the station or the Citadel Gate, should do justice to the rich history of Den Bosch.

207248.explanation: School swimming can be done without municipal funding.

207249.explanation: When safety in certain neighborhoods is at risk, FVD wants more community enforcement officers or police on the streets, especially in problem areas. However, community officers are often (too) frequently deployed to issue parking fines, for example.

207251.explanation: 'Youth work' may sound good, but it costs taxpayers a lot of money so that (problematic) youth receive daytime activities and extra help in life, for example with housing and work. FVD wants equal treatment, no preferential approach for any group.

207252.explanation: Camera surveillance is a superficial solution to nuisance. Often the police no longer even request camera footage due to lack of manpower or time. FVD does not want expensive investments in equipment but less bureaucracy so that officers have more time to spend in the neighborhoods.

207253.explanation: Homes in the municipality of 's-Hertogenbosch should primarily be allocated to our own people.

207254.explanation: Agreed, provided not too much nature or agricultural land has to be sacrificed. Tailored solutions are important here.

207255.explanation: Stimulation is fine, but as far as FVD is concerned, never compulsion or pressure. The housing shortage should not be solved by forcing elderly people out of their homes, but by addressing immigration.

207256.explanation: Vacancy rates in our municipality are relatively low. There is no need to introduce a new tax.

207257.explanation: No high-rise buildings where it does not fit. We do not build massive apartment blocks and towers that harm the livability or character of the environment, and which are also less dense forms of construction than traditional residential blocks.

207260.explanation: We do not support a self-occupancy obligation.

207261.explanation: A neighborhood that needs renovation more than others deserves to be served first.

207262.explanation: Everything the municipality gives away 'for free' is ultimately paid for by the taxpayer.

207266.explanation: FVD does NOT want zero-emission zones in the municipality.

207267.explanation: At present, the city of 's-Hertogenbosch has a car-restricted inner city with limited but sufficient accessibility. That should remain so.

207274.explanation: We support extensive freedom of choice in healthcare.

207275.explanation: We strive to preserve local and regional healthcare institutions, general practitioner posts, consultation offices, and hospitals. It is very important that residents can access necessary facilities in their own area. We promote and create space for small-scale and local healthcare initiatives.

207221.explanation: KA’26 fully supports parliamentary democracy and is therefore against what are called “democratic innovations.” The highest governing body of the municipality is the council, and this should remain so. However, we do support thorough and well-supported citizen participation in decision-making.

207222.explanation: KA’26 fully supports parliamentary democracy and is therefore against what are called “democratic innovations.” The highest governing body of the municipality is the council, and this should remain so. However, we do support thorough and well-supported citizen participation in decision-making.

207223.explanation: KA’26 wants to strengthen the position of the municipal ombuds committee to better protect citizens against government power. However, we do not see value in an ombudsperson who serves only the elderly.

207224.explanation: KA’26 believes that residents of our neighborhoods and villages know best what they need. We therefore think that the role of resident advisory groups (BAG) should be revised to encourage low-threshold citizen participation.

207225.explanation: KA’26 values business activity and the jobs it creates. However, due to the worsening housing crisis, we choose to prioritize housing construction over business development in the allocation of building land in coming years.

207226.explanation: KA’26 wants to create space for entrepreneurs and not stifle them with regulatory pressure. We believe the municipality should not be the 'teacher's pet' by imposing heavier rules on top of existing national obligations.

207227.explanation: KA’26 wants to keep the waste disposal tax for all households at the current level. We do want to maintain exemptions for the waste disposal tax for low-income households.

207229.explanation: KA’26 does not see advertising in public spaces as inherently undesirable.

207231.explanation: KA’26 sees the dog tax as an unfair tax on dog owners compared to other pet owners. Furthermore, the dog tax is poorly enforceable and costs too much money to enforce.

207232.explanation: KA’26 believes there is currently sufficient space on industrial estates in our municipality for businesses. Therefore, there is no need to designate additional business parks.

207233.explanation: KA’26 stands for fair treatment of labor migrants. Therefore, we support increased supervision of the (sometimes appalling) housing conditions of this group.

207234.explanation: KA’26 favors small-scale and dispersed shelter in the municipality. We believe this is the best way to limit nuisance. Therefore, we oppose the establishment of the Kruithoorn asylum seeker center due to its location and size.

207235.explanation: KA’26 wants to include in performance agreements with housing corporations that status holders are no longer treated as a priority group in the allocation of social rental housing. In the current situation, regular housing seekers on waiting lists are permanently disadvantaged. This is unfair.

207237.explanation: KA’26 wants to tackle waste dumping near shopping centers such as the Rompert. We want to allocate community enforcement officers who apply the same approach as was used to address the waste-gull problem in Leiden.

207238.explanation: KA’26 stands for a lively and vibrant city center. The city center is our shared living room, not a private residential area. We see no need to reduce the number of large events.

207239.explanation: KA’26 sees benefits both for the aesthetics of public spaces and climate-adaptive urban development in strongly greening our municipality.

207240.explanation: KA'26 supports a vibrant city center and lively nightlife. Therefore, we want to extend the closing hours in certain areas.

207241.explanation: KA’26 values clean and orderly neighborhoods. Clean neighborhoods contribute to a pleasant living environment for residents. We are willing to invest in this.

207242.explanation: KA’26 believes that “sustainability” should not be the only administrative criterion against which policies are tested. Furthermore, sustainability is an irrelevant issue in some policy domains.

207243.explanation: KA’26 believes that the municipality currently invests sufficiently in sports facilities. However, we assess this on an individual basis per facility.

207244.explanation: KA’26 believes that sharing sports facilities is not a necessary principle for municipal sports policy. We do recognize the value of having “one’s own home base.”

207245.explanation: KA’26 believes that cultural institutions contribute to community life and the enjoyment of our residents. That is worth investing in. However, we want to stop funding money-draining cultural prestige projects such as the Theater aan de Parade. We want to privatize these.

207246.explanation: KA’26 sees the importance of social-cultural meeting places in the neighborhoods. We believe these facilities must not be missing in new neighborhoods.

207247.explanation: KA’26 is strongly opposed to yet another money-draining architectural prestige project in the municipality, such as the Theater aan de Parade. We think that a “showpiece” at the Citadel Gate is nothing but a potential disaster.

207248.explanation: KA’26 believes every child in the Netherlands must be able to swim. After all, we are a water nation. The tragic drownings in recent years in the region have clearly highlighted the necessity of school swimming. We want to work on this together with other municipalities.

207249.explanation: KA’26 stands for a safe living environment, and the visibility of BOAs (Special Enforcement Officers) contributes to that. We want to invest in this.

207250.explanation: KA’26 sees no benefit in making Cold War-era shelters operational again. We advocate following the recommendations of the national government regarding emergency kits.

207251.explanation: KA’26 believes that more funding for youth work does not lead to better participation. KA’26 advocates for a new youth policy that fits the current times. Therefore, we want to modernize the youth ambassador project for the coming years.

207252.explanation: KA’26 sees camera surveillance in places with structural and severe nuisance as an effective and proportionate means to reduce disturbances. However, we want to guard against surveillance with an unnecessary permanent character.

207253.explanation: KA’26 understands that residents wish to continue living near friends and family within the municipality. Our preference is that these people can remain living in the ’s-Hertogenbosch municipality with social housing.

207254.explanation: KA’26 wants to tackle the housing crisis. This will demand the already limited space in our municipality. Therefore, we advocate allocating that space to housing, not to projects like the De Duurzame Polder wind farm.

207255.explanation: KA’26 believes that people themselves are responsible for financing their move.

207256.explanation: KA’26 sees the need to tackle vacancy, but does not view fines on vacant “properties” as the solution. No distinction is made here between buildings with a residential function or other functions.

207257.explanation: KA’26 believes that high-rise buildings will also be part of the solution to the housing crisis, but we are very cautious about building them in outer neighborhoods.

207259.explanation: KA’26 believes that the current target of 30% social housing is sufficient (current policy). However, KA’26 wants to promote homeownership for starters and young families.

207260.explanation: KA’26 finds a mandatory owner-occupation policy undesirable and unfeasible. However, we advocate strong protection against buyouts by “private equity” parties in the municipality that try to exploit the housing crisis.

207261.explanation: KA’26 sees the necessity to invest in vulnerable neighborhoods, but this must be proportional to the neighborhood itself and not relative to other neighborhoods.

207262.explanation: KA’26 supports the initiative of the Bossche Pas and sees it as a means to contribute to the subsistence security of less fortunate residents.

207263.explanation: KA’26 would like to remind people of the child benefit scandal. We believe the most vulnerable citizens deserve our protection and should not be treated as potential fraudsters without justification.

207264.explanation: KA’26 believes that meaningful paid work should be the ultimate goal for people in long-term unemployment. To reach that point, there is a process, and the municipality must have patience, but it is a desirable endpoint for people’s reintegration.

207265.explanation: KA’26 understands the need of people with modest incomes to use existing poverty schemes but nevertheless sees the necessity to enforce an upper limit to control costs.

207266.explanation: KA’26 sees the zero-emission zone in the city center as a failed experiment in symbolic politics that only causes trouble for entrepreneurs. As far as we are concerned, this measure should be reversed as soon as possible.

207267.explanation: KA’26 believes the city center must remain accessible to cars so that people with mobility issues are not solely dependent on public transport.

207268.explanation: KA’26 wants to address congestion in and around Rosmalen and sees the Eastern Ring Road as a measure that contributes sustainably to this.

207269.explanation: KA’26 thinks a train station at Maaspoort contributes to the attractiveness and livability of Maaspoort, Noord, and Empel. We wholeheartedly support this.

207271.explanation: KA’26 believes the inner-city ring road must remain passable for traffic. Nevertheless, we advocate for faster and better alternative routes for motorists.

207272.explanation: KA’26 sees fast bicycle connections as a desirable development. However, we advocate ensuring social safety along these bicycle paths.

207274.explanation: KA’26 believes that care recipients always have the final say about who helps them, but believes people do not have the right to demand a caregiver of the same background as themselves.

207275.explanation: KA’26 believes that neighborhood-bound caregivers do not necessarily provide better care but often are more expensive.

207276.explanation: KA’26 is grateful for all the work done by informal caregivers. We want to help them not by investing more money but by saving unnecessary costs.

207277.explanation: KA’26 believes that current welfare provisions are well capable of meeting residents’ requests for assistance. Any expansion or restriction of welfare provisions should be proportional to the overall demand for care in the municipality.

207278.explanation: KA’26 supports retaining the subsidy scheme “Goed voor elkaar” (“Good for each other”).

207279.explanation: KA’26 believes there are currently sufficient meeting places for the elderly in the municipality.

207280.explanation: KA’26 is not familiar with the individual case details of the Animal Shelter and therefore does not wish to comment on them.

207221.explanation: Residents should be able to participate in important local issues through referenda. In plans like those in the Rosmalense Polder, with wind turbines and area development, many residents feel unheard. Referenda give residents real influence on decisions that directly affect their living environment and their future!

207222.explanation: As Seniors ’s-Hertogenbosch, we believe it is important that residents contribute their thoughts on serious issues affecting the entire municipality through citizen assemblies. Citizen assemblies provide trust, involvement, and help the council make better decisions for all residents.

207223.explanation: We want a senior ombudsman so that seniors can be better assisted with complex municipal rules and applications. Many seniors get lost, rules are unknown or too complicated, causing Wmo assistance and other rights to remain unused and they fall below the social minimum.

207224.explanation: We want residents to have a say in district and village budgets. This increases involvement and ensures money is spent on what residents truly find important.

207225.explanation: We believe that when allocating building land, companies that truly create jobs for local residents should be given priority. This strengthens local employment, provided the criteria are clear, objective, and transparent.

207226.explanation: As Seniors ’s-Hertogenbosch, we believe companies should only follow national rules. Additional municipal rules should only apply in exceptional situations. This allows entrepreneurs to operate freely and without unnecessary bureaucracy.

207227.explanation: Single-person households should pay less for waste disposal fees. The principle 'polluter pays' should apply: those who produce more waste pay more. This makes the fee fair and encourages waste reduction.

207229.explanation: We disagree with having less advertising in public spaces. Advertising strengthens the local economy, stimulates culture, provides information to residents and tourists, and supports entrepreneurs and events, keeping the city lively and well-informed.

207231.explanation: We support the gradual phasing out of dog taxes. Dogs help seniors combat loneliness and isolation. They provide structure, exercise, and companionship. Therefore, the municipality should recognize this value and not burden dog owners with extra fees.

207232.explanation: We believe the municipality should create more business parks to strengthen the economy and employment. Local entrepreneurs should have priority in allocation, considering accessibility, traffic, and living environment, to keep jobs local.

207233.explanation: We believe the municipality should enforce stricter controls on abuses in the housing of labor migrants, as well as other forms of housing and business management, where necessary and based on well-founded reasons. This protects residents and keeps supervision fair and responsible.

207234.explanation: We believe asylum reception should be spread across the municipality so that not one or two neighborhoods become overloaded. A maximum of 600 places is allowed, and clustering is only permitted if facilities cannot otherwise be properly organized.

207235.explanation: We believe status holders should not be given priority for social rental housing. Our children and grandchildren sometimes wait up to ten years; everyone should have equal chances and fair waiting times.

207237.explanation: Illegal dumping of waste must be tackled more strictly to protect the living environment and safety. Clear fines and sufficient resources to punish offenders are needed. Facilitating bulky waste collection days by the waste disposal service can improve this, with feasible enforcement.

207238.explanation: Large events bring benefits to the city center, entrepreneurs, and employment. In consultation with organizers and residents, alternative locations can be considered to limit noise, traffic, and nuisance without significantly increasing the number of events.

207239.explanation: The municipality should invest more in green spaces in neighborhoods where needed. More greenery is welcome, provided it is well maintained and does not turn into uncontrolled overgrowth.

207240.explanation: Permitted opening hours of restaurants and cafés should be extended. Young people and residents deserve more freedom, but entrepreneurs should have a say in how long this is, ensuring it is feasible and responsible for the hospitality sector and surroundings.

207241.explanation: The municipality should spend more money on the maintenance of neighborhoods. Neglect is clearly visible in some neighborhoods. Proper maintenance is essential for livability, safety, and appreciation of the living environment.

207242.explanation: Policy should be assessed not only on sustainability but also on its impact on elderly care. This ensures livability now and supports the possibility of healthier aging.

207243.explanation: The municipality should invest more in sports facilities. Sports promote health, social cohesion, and livability. Accessible, senior-friendly facilities, an additional swimming pool, and swimming lessons for all children deserve extra attention and good maintenance.

207244.explanation: Sports halls, canteens, and fields should be used more often by multiple clubs to better utilize space. Clubs with enough activities and income may continue management, but cooperation and consultation with other users remain important.

207245.explanation: Cultural institutions in Den Bosch must continue to receive targeted funding. Budget cuts do not fit with collaboration, renewal, and quality. Funds must be used effectively, without waste, focusing on effectiveness, impact, and accessibility for all residents.

207246.explanation: New residential neighborhoods should always include a social cultural center. This can be combined with music, social activities, and a municipal desk for services and care, to optimally utilize space and promote livability, cohesion, and participation.

207247.explanation: The municipality should develop a new public attraction at the Citadel Gate, with realistic, feasible, and enjoyable functions, and enhance parking garage accessibility. Support from residents and entrepreneurs is important, and the project should not harm livability.

207248.explanation: The municipality should fund school swimming. As a water country, swimming is a vital skill for every child. Investing in swimming lessons protects children, increases safety and health, and prevents accidents in and around water.

207249.explanation: More enforcement officers (boa’s) should be deployed in ’s-Hertogenbosch. Increased supervision improves safety, order, and compliance, helps reduce nuisance, and ensures residents and entrepreneurs feel protected and heard in their neighborhood.

207250.explanation: The municipality should provide shelters, emergency supplies, and conduct trainings, drills, and simulations for crisis situations. This provides clarity, increases the resilience of residents, including seniors and people with disabilities, and reduces risks so everyone is safe and prepared.

207251.explanation: Strong youth work prevents problems, increases opportunities, and maintains livable neighborhoods. Through street presence, guidance, and activities, young people are reached and supported early. This is an investment in safety and the future.

207252.explanation: More camera surveillance is desirable in locations with proven nuisance, but only where truly needed. Surveillance must remain effective and proportional, with clear privacy safeguards. This strengthens security without losing sight of residents' rights.

207253.explanation: Local housing applicants should keep priority as much as possible for social rental housing. Our children and grandchildren sometimes wait more than ten years. Local priority helps improve turnover and keeps our community livable and stable.

207254.explanation: New homes in rural areas are welcome, but only with preservation of village character, suitable facilities, and sufficient public transport. This allows growth, maintains livability, and preserves the identity of villages for current and future residents.

207255.explanation: Seniors who want to downsize receive a moving allowance as an incentive. This promotes turnover and increases the housing supply. This aligns with the principles of a senior-friendly municipality for independence, social participation, and age-proof living.

207256.explanation: Owners of vacant properties should not automatically pay extra taxes. This may discourage owners, raise rents, or fail to solve vacancy. Only in cases of long-term, deliberate neglect without reason can an incentive truly improve livability and housing access.

207257.explanation: The municipality should allow more high-rise buildings outside the historic city center to accommodate the huge housing shortage in a growing city. This may only be done if livability and facilities in the neighborhood are maintained so that living remains pleasant for everyone.

207259.explanation: New homes must include sufficient social rental units, but the balance with owner-occupied homes must not be lost. The national average is 28%. New construction should remain affordable for everyone while guaranteeing enough social rental housing.

207260.explanation: Buyers of homes in ’s-Hertogenbosch must live in the property themselves for five years. This prevents quick renting out by investors and helps keep homes fairly available for residents, starters, and families, allowing exceptions based on special personal circumstances.

207261.explanation: Vulnerable neighborhoods receive extra attention through smart and targeted spending on safety, support, and involved residents. This addresses problems, increases individual responsibility, and keeps the neighborhood safe, livable, and strong.

207262.explanation: The Bossche Pas currently offers discounts and credits for sports, culture, and recreation, especially for low-income people. The municipality should expand this with additional social arrangements such as transport, energy, or education so more residents can participate and access facilities.

207263.explanation: Control of abuse of poverty schemes must be done carefully, respecting vulnerable people. Strict, routine checks can increase stress, distrust, and bureaucracy. Enforcement must be proportional, with clear rules and people-oriented support.

207264.explanation: Seniors ’s-Hertogenbosch believes support for the unemployed should mainly focus on paid work. With reintegration programs, job coaching, trial placements, and subsidies for retraining, together with entrepreneurs, skills are strengthened, self-confidence maintained, and jobs created.

207265.explanation: More low-income people should be able to use poverty schemes. Schemes must be accessible and easy to find, with clear information and simple application processes, so everyone receives what they are entitled to without stress or bureaucracy.

207266.explanation: The zero-emission zone in the city center should be reconsidered. Not everyone can invest in clean cars, it burdens entrepreneurs, and achieves hardly any environmental benefits. Affordable, effective alternatives and gradual implementation are better.

207267.explanation: The city center of ’s-Hertogenbosch must remain accessible by car. Seniors and residents with mobility impairments must be able to shop and enjoy the market. Entrepreneurs, such as wine shops, need parking for loading, unloading, and deliveries. This keeps the city livable and welcoming.

207268.explanation: The municipality must ensure better eastern access for Rosmalen and the Groote Wielen to improve growth, traffic flow, and livability. Plans must be carefully coordinated with safety, environment, and living quality in mind.

207269.explanation: The municipality must strive for better public transport for Maaspoort and other neighborhoods. The focus is on improved bus lines, bicycle connections, and public transport links, as a new train station near Maaspoort is not feasible in the short term.

207271.explanation: The municipality should not restrict through car traffic on the inner-city ring road. Seniors, disabled individuals, entrepreneurs, and visitors need access by car. Restrictions lead to congestion on other roads and can harm accessibility and the economy. Smart traffic and parking policies are preferable.

207272.explanation: Fast bicycle connections are important, but existing bike paths must first be made safer, wider, and better maintained. Seniors use bicycles or mobility scooters, children bike to school; safety is paramount. Fatbikes should be banned to reduce congestion and danger.

207274.explanation: People should receive care based on quality, expertise, and availability, not on religion, culture, or beliefs. This keeps care accessible, efficient, and equal for everyone.

207275.explanation: We support permanent care professionals in every neighborhood because this improves care quality, provides certainty to seniors and vulnerable residents, and enables early detection. When absent or replaced, a reliable substitute must always be available.

207276.explanation: We support more funding for informal caregivers. Those who care for family at home and as a result work less should receive appropriate compensation. Additional support can include respite care, a parking permit at their home address, and other practical help.

207277.explanation: Conversely: Welfare provisions such as community centers, activities, and social support are crucial for seniors, vulnerable residents, social cohesion, and livability. They prevent isolation and support independence and participation in the neighborhood.

207278.explanation: Neighborhood initiatives that increase contact between residents are important for social cohesion. Additional subsidies can be applied for through neighborhood or village budgets, giving initiatives extra support when needed.

207279.explanation: Meeting places for seniors deserve more investment. This aligns with the principles of a senior-friendly city: social participation and inclusion, reducing loneliness, and promoting seniors’ well-being, health, and involvement.

207280.explanation: The municipality must co-finance the expansion of the animal shelter. This improves animal welfare, prevents nuisance, and increases community involvement, ensuring pets are well cared for and safely housed.

statements.title: The number of major events in the city center of 's-Hertogenbosch should be limited.
statements.moreInfo: Large events are organized every year in the city center of ’s-Hertogenbosch. Examples are carnival, Jazz in Duketown, the eleventh of the eleventh and Theatrefestival Boulevard. At the same time many people live and work in the city center.
statements.pro: Large events cause a lot of nuisance for residents. The city center must remain liveable. Fewer events provide more peace and space for living and working.
statements.con: Events are important for the culture and identity of the city. Events attract visitors and revenue. Large events make the city vibrant and attractive.

statements.title: The municipality should appoint an ombudsman for the elderly.

titleParts.glossary: An independent person who helps residents with complaints about the government.

statements.moreInfo: An ombudsman is an independent person who helps residents with complaints about the government. The ombudsman listens to complaints, gives advice and can mediate between residents and the government. The aim is to resolve problems fairly and carefully. In ’s-Hertogenbosch there is already an Ombudsman Committee that handles complaints about municipal officials and administrators. The municipality can choose to appoint a separate ombudsman for older people.
statements.pro: Older people need one clear point of contact. Older people are vulnerable. It helps elderly people who are not familiar with the rules and agencies.
statements.con: Older people can go to the existing desks. A new position costs extra money. A separate ombudsman makes the system more complicated.

statements.title: There should be less advertising in public spaces.

titleParts.text: Er moet minder reclame komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Outdoor areas that are accessible to everyone, such as streets, squares, and parks.

statements.moreInfo: Advertising is visible in public spaces, such as on bus shelters, billboards and digital screens. The municipality has contracts with companies that enable advertising on, for example, lampposts and locations along streets.
statements.pro: Less advertising creates a calmer streetscape. Advertising contributes to overconsumption, meaning people buy things they don't need, which mainly causes waste. Advertising screens with lighting use a lot of energy.
statements.con: Too many rules restrict entrepreneurs. Businesses should be able to promote themselves. Advertising generates income for the municipality. Advertising is part of a lively city.

statements.title: More business parks should be developed in the municipality.
statements.moreInfo: A business park is an area with no homes but mainly companies, such as offices, factories and storage facilities.
statements.pro: Businesses must be given room to grow. More business parks create more jobs. A strong economy is good for the municipality.
statements.con: Space can be made on existing business parks. More companies cause extra traffic and pressure on facilities. Space is scarce. The space should be used for housing or nature.

statements.title: Asylum accommodation should be distributed across multiple locations in the municipality.
statements.moreInfo: People who flee their country because of war or personal persecution can apply for asylum in the Netherlands. The Immigration and Naturalisation Service (IND) assesses their application and decides whether they may stay. During the procedure these asylum seekers must be accommodated. The Netherlands currently has a shortage of reception places for asylum seekers. The municipality of ’s-Hertogenbosch has plans for one central reception center for 600 asylum seekers at the Groot Meerendonk construction site. The discussion is whether it would be better to have multiple small reception locations instead of one central center.
statements.pro: Distribution prevents one neighborhood from being overburdened. Small-scale reception facilities enable better guidance and integration. Multiple locations ensure that more residents support the reception.
statements.con: One central shelter is clearer and easier to manage. Spreading it out causes more nuisance in multiple places. Multiple locations make sheltering more expensive and complicated.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.moreInfo: Residents with a dog must pay a dog tax in ’s-Hertogenbosch. The municipality can use the revenue for dog facilities, such as dog fields and cleaning up dog waste, but it can also spend the revenue on other things. The dog tax for 2026 is €108 per year for the first dog.
statements.pro: The money is not used for dog facilities. The dog tax is unfair. There is no tax on other pets. More and more municipalities are abolishing the dog tax.
statements.con: Dog owners cause additional costs in public spaces. The dog tax provides revenue for the municipality. Abolishing it means other taxes must rise or cuts must be made.

statements.title: For important issues the municipality should ask residents for their opinion via a referendum.

titleParts.text: Bij belangrijke onderwerpen moet de gemeente de inwoners om hun mening vragen via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.moreInfo: A referendum is a way for residents to express their opinion on an important issue. There is already a referendum arrangement in ’s-Hertogenbosch, but it is almost never used. A referendum only takes place if a majority of the municipal council wants it.
statements.pro: A referendum gives residents more say. Important decisions thus gain more support from residents. It increases engagement with local politics.
statements.con: Decisions are already made by elected representatives. A referendum can oversimplify a complex issue. Residents should be involved in other ways, for example through a citizens' council.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on green spaces in the neighborhoods.
statements.moreInfo: Green in the neighborhoods consists of trees, plants, parks and green spaces. The municipality of ’s-Hertogenbosch is responsible for creating and maintaining green in the neighborhoods.
statements.pro: Green spaces provide a healthy and pleasant living environment. More greenery helps against heat and water nuisance. Greenery is important for social contact and wellbeing in the neighborhood.
statements.con: More greenery costs the municipality extra money. Residents can take the initiative themselves for more green space. More green often comes at the expense of parking spaces.

statements.title: Cultural institutions should receive less funding from the municipality.
statements.moreInfo: Municipalities provide funding to cultural institutions, such as theatres, museums, libraries and pop venues. This money is used for buildings, staff and activities for residents, for example.
statements.pro: Cultural institutions should earn as much of their own money as possible. Municipal funds are needed for other tasks. Subsidies make institutions dependent on the government.
statements.con: Culture connects people. Culture should be accessible to everyone. Subsidies can keep ticket prices lower. Cultural institutions provide vibrancy and appeal.

statements.title: The municipality of 's-Hertogenbosch should assess all policies for sustainability.

titleParts.glossary: A plan for how a problem will be addressed.
Sustainability means meeting our needs without harming future generations.

statements.moreInfo: In ’s-Hertogenbosch a sustainability statement is already mandatory for every council proposal that concerns the physical environment and assets you can see and use, such as buildings, roads, parks and nature. This sustainability statement explains how the proposal contributes to the ambition of becoming climate-neutral or moving to a circular economy. Climate-neutral means the municipality emits no or almost no greenhouse gases that warm the climate, such as CO2. A circular economy means waste is reused as much as possible. Should the municipality also make such a statement mandatory for all other proposals?
statements.pro: Sustainability assessments help prevent negative impacts on the climate and environment. Policy choices become more future-proof as a result. The municipality sets a good example.
statements.con: Additional checks can slow down decision-making. Other interests, such as the economy or livability, must also be taken into account. National sustainability policy is sufficient.

statements.title: The municipality should ensure that sports halls, sports canteens, and sports fields are used more often by multiple clubs.
statements.moreInfo: Sports halls, sports fields and clubhouses are often owned by the municipality. Sports clubs use these facilities. By sharing facilities, more clubs can play there. That requires clear agreements about schedules, maintenance and use. The municipality can help make these agreements.
statements.pro: This allows more people to exercise. It prevents the need to build additional sports facilities. This saves costs.
statements.con: For sports clubs, having their own fixed location is important. The municipality should not impose cooperation. A sports club should not be located in multiple places in the city.

statements.title: The municipality should work toward a train station at Maaspoort.
statements.moreInfo: Maaspoort is a residential neighbourhood in ’s-Hertogenbosch. The municipality would like a train station in Maaspoort. A station could improve accessibility of the neighbourhood. NS and the national government have previously said they do not want to invest in a Maaspoort station. If the municipality still wants a station, it must lobby NS and the national government.
statements.pro: A station improves the accessibility of Maaspoort. More people can then travel sustainably. It reduces car traffic and parking pressure.
statements.con: Without support from ProRail, NS and the national government, a train station is not feasible. The municipality should focus on better bus service between neighborhoods. Improving the central station in 's-Hertogenbosch is more important.

statements.title: People granted refugee status should no longer be given priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Statushouders mogen geen voorrang meer krijgen op

titleParts.glossary: A rental home that at the start of the tenancy is not more expensive than a certain amount. In 2026 that is €932.93 per month.

statements.moreInfo: Status holders are people who applied for asylum and have been granted a residence permit. They are allowed to stay in the Netherlands. The national government determines every six months how many status holders a municipality must house; this number is based on the municipality’s population. Municipalities may decide themselves who gets priority for a social rental home. Status holders do not automatically get priority; it depends on the municipality’s rules. In ’s-Hertogenbosch status holders are one of the groups that receive priority when social rental homes are allocated.
statements.pro: Priority for status holders is unfair to other home-seekers. Waiting time should be the main criterion for everyone. Priority rules may prevent young people from finding a home in their own city.
statements.con: Status holders need housing quickly to be able to participate. Municipalities have a legal duty to house status holders. Without priority, an accommodation problem may arise.

statements.title: Owners of properties that remain vacant for a long time should be required to pay a tax.
statements.moreInfo: A municipality can introduce a vacancy tax on homes or commercial buildings that have been unused for a long time. The aim is to encourage owners to rent out or sell the buildings. In ’s-Hertogenbosch the vacancy rate is 2%, the same as the average in North Brabant.
statements.pro: Vacancy is undesirable in a housing shortage. A vacancy tax encourages owners to rent out or develop properties. A vacancy tax encourages owners to maintain their buildings better.
statements.con: Additional taxation is too harsh a measure. The owner cannot always do anything about a building standing empty. Encouragement and cooperation work better than punishment.

statements.title: The municipality should develop a new visitor attraction at the Citadelpoort.
statements.moreInfo: New construction will take place in the Citadelpoort, an area between the Aa, the Orthenseweg and the Citadellaan in ’s-Hertogenbosch. In addition to housing, the municipality also wanted to build the new Design Museum here. Due to high costs that museum construction will not go ahead. The municipality has not yet decided whether Citadelpoort will now contain only housing or also a museum or another public attraction.
statements.pro: A visitor attraction brings more visitors to the city center. It strengthens the economy and employment. The area becomes livelier and more attractive.
statements.con: The development costs the municipality a lot of money. There should primarily be space for housing. A new major attraction is not necessary.

statements.title: More enforcement officers (BOAs) should be employed in the municipality of 's-Hertogenbosch.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

statements.moreInfo: Enforcement officers (BOAs) provide supervision and enforcement in public spaces in ’s-Hertogenbosch. They address people causing nuisance, check whether rules are followed and work together with the police. The municipality of ’s-Hertogenbosch currently has 48 BOAs.
statements.pro: More enforcers increase safety on the streets. This boosts residents' sense of security. Enforcement officers can act more quickly in cases of nuisance and minor offences.
statements.con: Prevention and education are more important than enforcement. What is mainly needed is more police. BOAs are too often used to issue parking fines.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more high-rise buildings outside the historic city center of 's-Hertogenbosch.

titleParts.glossary: Buildings with homes or businesses on multiple floors, for example apartments or flats.

titleParts.text: toestaan buiten de historische binnenstad van 's-Hertogenbosch.

statements.pro: With high-rise buildings many homes can be built in a short time. It prevents parks, nature areas or the countryside from being built over. High-rise building allows more homes to be built on a smaller piece of land.
statements.con: High-rise buildings can make neighborhoods look and feel different for residents. They can cause shadowing and loss of view for people in other homes. Not every location is suitable for tall buildings.

statements.title: The municipality's support for the unemployed should be primarily aimed at finding paid employment.
statements.moreInfo: Municipalities support people without work, for example with guidance, training or benefits. The aim is for people to participate in society as well as possible. The municipality can choose to focus support mainly on finding paid work, or also on activities such as volunteer work.
statements.pro: Paid work increases independence. Employment is the best way to get off welfare. Assistance from the municipality should encourage people to look for work.
statements.con: Not everyone can do paid work. Other forms of participation are also valuable. Support should primarily focus on solving underlying problems.

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
statements.moreInfo: In some municipalities buyers of a home must live in it themselves; this is called an owner-occupation obligation. Since 1 July 2022 ’s-Hertogenbosch has had purchase-protection (opkoopbescherming). This is a form of owner-occupation obligation where a house may not be rented out after purchase. This rule currently applies to homes with a WOZ value below €260,000 on 1 January 2020.
statements.pro: Houses are for living in, not for making a profit. An owner-occupancy requirement gives first-time buyers a better chance at buying a home. Neighborhoods remain liveable and social. People who live in a place themselves more often take care of contacts and involvement.
statements.con: People should be free to decide what to do with their home. More rules will not solve the housing shortage. Without investors, fewer rental homes will come onto the market.

statements.title: The municipality should strictly check for abuse of poverty assistance schemes.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet streng controleren op misbruik van

titleParts.glossary: Programs that help low-income people with extra support.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities have poverty schemes to support residents with low incomes, for example with money, discounts or services. The municipality of ’s-Hertogenbosch has, for example, special assistance (bijzondere bijstand). This is a reimbursement for necessary costs that someone cannot pay themselves.
statements.pro: Municipal funds must be spent carefully. Abuse is unfair to people who genuinely need the schemes. Strict checks create trust in the system.
statements.con: Most people do not abuse the system. Trust should be the starting point. Strict checks can deter people from asking for help. Controls take a lot of time and money and come at the expense of assistance.

statements.title: Vulnerable neighborhoods should receive more funding than other neighborhoods.
statements.moreInfo: Some neighbourhoods have more problems than others, such as poverty, unemployment and insecurity. These are called vulnerable neighbourhoods. The municipality can choose to invest more money in these neighbourhoods, for example in housing, safety and local facilities.
statements.pro: Problems are greater in vulnerable neighborhoods. Equal opportunities require unequal effort. Some neighborhoods need more support to catch up. More money for vulnerable neighborhoods increases liveability.
statements.con: All neighborhoods should be treated equally. More money does not automatically solve problems. Each neighborhood has its own issues.

statements.title: New homes should be built in the rural areas.

titleParts.glossary: Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.

statements.moreInfo: There are too few homes in ’s-Hertogenbosch. The municipality must choose where new homes will be built. That can be within the existing urban area, for example on empty plots or by replacing old buildings. The municipality can also choose new neighbourhoods outside the built-up area, in the countryside. The countryside lies outside the towns and city and mainly consists of agriculture, nature and open space.
statements.pro: There is a severe housing shortage. Building in the rural outskirts provides more space for new homes. New housing can contribute to the liveability of villages.
statements.con: Building in the countryside comes at the expense of nature and the landscape. Open space must be protected. Development should first take place within towns and villages.

statements.title: The municipality should expand the Bossche Pas with additional measures.
statements.moreInfo: With the Bossche Pas, residents of ’s-Hertogenbosch can participate in sports, culture and other activities at a discount or for free since January 2026. The pass is available to everyone. For residents with a low income the pass is free. The municipality can choose to expand the pass with more schemes and offers.
statements.pro: The Bossche Pas helps low-income people participate. More schemes create equal opportunities for all residents. Expanding the pass provides clarity for residents.
statements.con: Expansion costs the municipality extra money. First it must be clear whether the Bossche Pas is working well. The system is too complicated, making implementation expensive and leaving less money for residents.

statements.title: The municipality should reverse the decision to introduce a zero-emission zone in the city center of 's-Hertogenbosch.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het besluit terugdraaien over de instelling van een

titleParts.glossary: An area in a city where cars that emit harmful pollutants are not allowed.

statements.moreInfo: A zero-emission zone has applied in the city centre of ’s-Hertogenbosch since 1 March 2025. In principle only electric commercial vehicles may drive there. In special cases an exemption is possible. These rules do not apply to passenger cars; they may still enter the city centre. The aim is to improve air quality and reduce the emission of harmful substances.
statements.pro: The decision is too burdensome for entrepreneurs. An electric company vehicle is expensive and not affordable for everyone. Reversing the decision prevents uncertainty and extra costs.
statements.con: The zero-emission zone provides cleaner air and better health. The decision gives clarity for the future. Reversing it would create unreliable policy.

statements.title: The municipality should invest more money in youth work.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld steken in

titleParts.glossary: People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.moreInfo: Youth work provides support for young people and young adults, usually between 12 and 27 years old, for example through street work, youth centres and guidance. Youth workers are active on the street, at school, online and in youth centres. They help young people with school, work, leisure and personal problems. They also have contact with parents, schools and care providers. The municipality is responsible for organizing and paying for youth work.
statements.pro: Youth work prevents problems such as nuisance and crime. Early help is better than intervening later. Youth work helps young people participate and develop.
statements.con: More youth work costs the municipality extra money. Youth work does not help enough. Young people who cause nuisance must be dealt with strictly. The municipality should focus on strengthening education.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in the municipality of 's-Hertogenbosch at locations where nuisance occurs.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

titleParts.text: komen op plekken waar overlast is.

statements.moreInfo: Camera surveillance is a tool the municipality can use to monitor nuisance and crime. Cameras are placed in public spaces and record what happens there. In ’s-Hertogenbosch there is currently permanent camera surveillance in a few places, such as the nightlife area and the station. The municipality also sometimes places temporary cameras where there is a lot of nuisance.
statements.pro: Camera surveillance helps tackle nuisance and crime. Cameras increase the feeling of safety. Footage can aid in investigation and enforcement.
statements.con: Cameras do not solve the causes of nuisance. There is already sufficient camera surveillance. Camera surveillance violates residents' privacy.

statements.title: The municipality should take measures to reduce through car traffic on the inner-city ring road.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet maatregelen nemen om doorgaand autoverkeer op de

titleParts.glossary: A road that runs around the city center.

statements.moreInfo: The inner-city ring in ’s-Hertogenbosch is used by traffic entering the city but also by through traffic. Especially during rush hours it is very busy and traffic comes to a standstill. The municipality can take measures to stop through traffic, making the ring less attractive as a through route.
statements.pro: Less through-traffic increases safety and liveability in the city center. It improves traffic flow for destination traffic. It encourages use of other routes or other modes of transport.
statements.con: Good accessibility for businesses and visitors is important. The measures are bad for the local economy. Preventing through-traffic leads to congestion on other roads.

statements.title: At least 40% of the new homes to be built in the municipality must be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in de gemeente moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: Social rental homes are intended for people with lower incomes. In ’s-Hertogenbosch the current policy is that at least 30% of new housing must be social rent. Of the existing homes just over 33% are social rental housing.
statements.pro: There is a large shortage of affordable housing. Low-income people thus get more opportunities for housing. Neighborhoods become more mixed with residents of lower and higher incomes.
statements.con: People with slightly higher incomes will find it harder to obtain housing. If mainly social rental homes and fewer higher-priced homes are added, it becomes harder for people to move to the next home. Applying the same standard to every new construction project leaves too little room for tailored solutions.

statements.title: People who need care or support should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.glossary: For example youth care, day activities, loan of a wheelchair, household help, transport, individual guidance, etc.

titleParts.text: of ondersteuning nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een hulpverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.moreInfo: For care and support from the municipality, such as domestic help or guidance, residents sometimes have a preference for a caregiver with a particular background. In the current care system the assignment of a caregiver does not take into account the cultural or religious background of the person receiving care.
statements.pro: Care should be person-centered. Self-direction is important in care and support. Care fits better when a person’s background is taken into account.
statements.con: Due to staff shortages, freedom of choice is not always feasible. Care is neutral: good care can also be provided without a shared background. Unlimited choice of any preferred provider is too expensive.

statements.title: The municipality should contribute financially to the expansion of the animal shelter.
statements.moreInfo: The animal shelter in ’s-Hertogenbosch takes in dogs and cats that have been found or are wandering. Each year this concerns more than 500 animals. On average there are about 70 animals in the shelter at the same time. The shelter wants to expand, but there is not enough money.
statements.pro: Animal welfare is important. The municipality is responsible for the reception of found animals. The animal shelter does not have enough space to properly accommodate all animals.
statements.con: Expansion costs the municipality extra money. Information campaigns should reduce the need for shelter. Ongoing support can lead other organizations to expect funding from the municipality as well.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should spend more money on the maintenance of neighborhoods.
's-Hertogenbosch should hold citizens' assemblies more often to advise the municipal council. A citizens' assembly consists of residents selected by lot.
When allocating building land, companies that provide jobs in the municipality should be given priority.
In new neighborhood developments there should always be a place for music and cultural associations.
Residents should be able to have a say in the budget for their neighborhood or village.
The municipality should not impose additional rules on companies beyond national regulations.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.
Single-person households should pay less for the waste disposal charges.

shootoutStatements.theme: Widening the eligibility for poverty assistance
shootoutStatements.title: More low-income people should be able to make use of poverty support schemes.
The municipality should more strictly monitor abuses in the housing of migrant workers.
Permitted opening hours for restaurants and cafés should be extended.
The municipality should invest more in fast cycling routes.
The municipality should invest more in sports facilities.
When allocating a social rental home, applicants from the municipality should continue to be given priority wherever possible.
Each neighborhood should have permanent care professionals who provide long-term support to residents.
Dumping of waste should be tackled more strictly.
The municipality should provide shelters and emergency stockpiles for crisis situations.
The city center of 's‑Hertogenbosch should also remain accessible by car.
Local initiatives that increase contact between residents should receive extra subsidies.
Seniors who move to a smaller home should receive a moving allowance.
There should be an eastern access road for Rosmalen and the eastern part of the municipality.
The municipality should provide more funding to support informal caregivers.
The municipality should stop expanding welfare services.
The municipality should invest more money in meeting places for older people.
